question_text: Planet X has a gravitational field strength of 20 N/kg at its surface, while Planet Y has a gravitational field strength of 5 N/kg at its surface. A 500 kg spacecraft lands on Planet X, then later lands on Planet Y. Calculate the weight of the spacecraft on each planet, and explain why a person would feel heavier on Planet X even though both planets might be the same size.
correct_answer: Planet X: 10000 N; Planet Y: 2500 N. A person feels heavier on Planet X because the gravitational field strength is stronger there, meaning gravity pulls down with greater force per kilogram of mass. This stronger field could be due to Planet X having greater mass or being more dense than Planet Y.
explanation: Weight on X = 500 × 20 = 10000 N; Weight on Y = 500 × 5 = 2500 N. Gravitational field strength depends on a planet's mass and size, so a stronger field (like Planet X's) indicates either greater mass or a smaller radius, both of which increase the pull of gravity.
